meaning: n.漫止,弯曲,曲流v.漫止,蜿蜒而流
examples: I liked to meander through familiar streets.
They usually meander down to the pub after work.
Then I meander down to the freshly swept beach and join the others for dinner.
Considering how many people meander around its displays, it is surprising how few objects are damaged.
Witnesses often meander through an answer, wandering around a question, but never really answering it.
